new_classes.label_en: Still fermented beverage from concentrated grape must in containers > 2 L (subheading 2206 00 89)
new_classes.label_de: Stilles gegorenes Getränk aus konzentriertem Traubenmost in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 L (Unterposition 2206 00 89)
new_classes.definition_en: Still (non-sparkling, non-semi-sparkling) fermented beverage produced from concentrated grape must (not the product of natural fermentation of must from fresh grapes), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres. The concentrated must is shelf-stable and may be stored; fermentation is typically induced subsequently.

coverage_explanation: The three principal discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the beverage is still/non-sparkling (StillFermentedBeverageQuality), (2) it is produced from concentrated grape must rather than natural fresh-grape fermentation (ConcentratedGrapeMustOriginQuality + ConcentratedGrapeMustFermentationProcess), and (3) it is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres (LargeContainer via BFO has_part). A disjointness complement against the sibling subheading 22060051 (small container, ≤ 2 L) is also asserted. Minor gap: the legal note alludes to the specific technological step by which fermentation of concentrated must is typically 'induced' (triggered later), which is descriptive context not fully formalised as a separate quality or process class to avoid over-commitment. The container volume threshold > 2 L is expressed via the existing eucn:LargeContainer class (≥ 2 L per TBox equivalence), which is a slight approximation (2 L exactly would belong to the small-container sibling), but this follows the existing TBox pattern.
